scenario: Polar Insurance models a Camunda 8.8 DMN decision that derives the current season from an input date for 6000 requests per day. The team replaces the original decision table with a single decision literal expression and must confirm which expression language is required inside the literalExpression text element before the decision is invoked from a business rule task.
question: Which expression language must the text body of a DMN literal expression be written in?

options.text: The text body of the literalExpression element uses JavaScript expressions when invoked from a business rule task
The text body of the literalExpression element uses Groovy expressions when invoked from a business rule task
The text body of the literalExpression element uses FEEL expressions when invoked from a business rule task
The text body of the literalExpression element uses XPath expressions when invoked from a business rule task

a.text: Incorrect. The DMN docs explicitly name FEEL as the expression language of a decision literal expression; JavaScript is not a literal expression language for Camunda DMN.

b.text: Incorrect. The docs name FEEL as the expression language inside the literalExpression text node; Groovy is not a supported literal expression language in Camunda DMN.

c.text: Correct. The DMN docs explicitly state that the expression language of the literal expression is FEEL and that the body of the expression sits inside the text element.

d.text: Incorrect. The docs require FEEL inside the literalExpression text element; XPath is not a DMN literal expression language in Camunda DMN.

explanation: A DMN decision literal expression carries a single FEEL expression inside the text child of the literalExpression element; FEEL is the only expression language documented for this form of decision.
